claim: Tim Cook privately believes the Vision Pro platform remains strategically important for Apple's long-term innovation narrative, despite weak sales. He sees a major visionOS update as necessary to signal commitment to developers and early adopters, and to maintain the platform's viability until futur
beliefs: visionOS is a foundational OS for spatial computing
abandoning now would damage Apple's reputation for long-term investment
goals: preserve optionality for future AR/glasses products
commitments: publicly stated Apple is 'all in' on spatial computing at 2024 shareholder meeting
pressures: Board concerns about R&D ROI; internal factions pushing to cut losses

relationships.marketing_team: delegates messaging but expects alignment with strategic narrative

historical_case_refs: Apple continued macOS development during PowerPC-to-Intel transition despite low sales
distinguishing_observations: Would personally approve keynote inclusion of visionOS 27
action_if_state: Direct marketing_team to include a visionOS 27 segment in the WWDC keynote, emphasizing new features and developer tools.
transition_triggers: New internal sales data showing uptick

claim: Tim Cook privately accepts that Vision Pro is a failed product and that further investment in visionOS is wasteful. He prioritizes reallocating engineering resources to AI and services, and views a major OS announcement as misleading to investors and developers. He prefers a quiet maintenance mode o
beliefs: Vision Pro is a niche dead-end
Apple must focus on AI to compete
commitments: internal memo (leaked) stating 'we must be ruthless with underperforming projects'
pressures: Analyst calls to cut losses; strong performance of Services division

relationships.marketing_team: instructed to downplay visionOS in favor of iOS/macOS AI features

historical_case_refs: Apple quietly discontinued the Xserve without a major OS update
distinguishing_observations: Would veto any mention of visionOS 27 in keynote or press release
action_if_state: Instruct marketing_team to omit visionOS 27 from WWDC keynote and issue no visionOS press release; instead, announce only minor visionOS 26.x updates.
assumptions: No contractual obligation to developers for a major update

claim: The marketing team has prepared a polished segment for the WWDC keynote announcing visionOS 27, including new features (e.g., improved spatial Persona, enterprise APIs) and a developer beta. They believe this aligns with Apple's tradition of annual OS updates and counters abandonment rumors.
beliefs: WWDC keynote must include all major OS updates
visionOS 27 is necessary to retain developer trust
commitments: internal schedule shows visionOS 27 segment rehearsed
pressures: Short keynote time; competition from iOS 27 and macOS 27 demos

relationships.tim_cook: seeks final approval but expects inclusion

historical_case_refs: WWDC 2025 included visionOS 26 despite low sales
action_if_state: Present visionOS 27 segment during WWDC keynote as planned, with demo and beta availability.
assumptions: Tim Cook does not veto at last minute

claim: The marketing team has been directed to remove any visionOS 27 content from the keynote and instead issue only a minor point update (visionOS 26.6) via press release. They believe this is to avoid highlighting a failing platform and to focus messaging on AI and services.
beliefs: visionOS is a liability for the keynote narrative
minor update suffices for existing users
goals: avoid negative attention on Vision Pro
commitments: final keynote script reviewed without visionOS 27
pressures: Strong directive from Tim Cook; limited time for other demos
historical_case_refs: WWDC 2023 did not announce a major watchOS update (only watchOS 9.x) when Apple Watch sales slumped
distinguishing_observations: No visionOS 27 slide in final keynote deck
action_if_state: Omit visionOS 27 from keynote; publish a brief press release on June 8 announcing visionOS 26.6 with bug fixes.
assumptions: No last-minute change from Tim Cook
